title: Thunderstorm, rain and heatwave alert in Rajasthan today: Mercury crosses 44°C, showers hit Bikaner, Hanuma... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
Thunderstorm, rain and heatwave alert in Rajasthan today:Mercury crosses 44°C; showers hit Bikaner, Hanumangarh and Sri Ganganagar
Rajasthan is witnessing a mix of intense heat and rising humidity. Temperatures in several western districts have crossed 44°C, while eastern Rajasthan, including Jaipur and Bharatpur divisions, remains below 40°C but is experiencing uncomfortable humidity and sultry conditions.
The Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for thunderstorms and rain in six districts on Monday. A heatwave warning has also been issued for two districts.
Phalodi and Sri Ganganagar remain the hottest
Western Rajasthan continued to record the highest temperatures on Sunday.
All remaining districts recorded maximum temperatures below 40°C.
Dust storms, rain and hail hit several areas
A weather change was seen across parts of Rajasthan on Sunday afternoon and evening.
Dust-laden winds swept through parts of Bikaner division, including Mahajan, as well as Nohar in Hanumangarh and several areas of Sri Ganganagar. Rain accompanied by strong winds was reported in parts of Nohar.
In Ghadsana, Sri Ganganagar district, hailstorms accompanied rain and strong winds. Nokha in Bikaner district also received heavy rain during a dust storm.
Evening showers were reported from parts of Alwar, Deeg and Barod. Light rain with thunder and lightning also occurred at isolated places in Chittorgarh and Pratapgarh districts.
Humidity increases discomfort in eastern districts
People faced humid conditions throughout the day in Tonk, Alwar, Ajmer, Bhilwara, Nagaur, Dausa, Karauli and Pratapgarh.
Tonk recorded significant rainfall over the past two days. Between the afternoon of June 6 and late Sunday night, the district received 93.5 mm of rain. Weak sunshine and low wind speed led to high humidity levels. The maximum temperature in Tonk was 37.2°C.
Other maximum temperatures recorded were:
City-wise weather report
Jaipur: Humidity dominates despite weak sunshine
Jaipur witnessed light cloud cover on Sunday, keeping sunshine weak for most of the day. However, humidity remained high and caused discomfort.
Ajmer: Heat likely to increase again
Clear skies and bright sunshine prevailed in Ajmer throughout Sunday.
The Meteorological Department expects dry weather for about a week, which could lead to a further rise in temperatures.
Kota: Mercury crosses 40°C
Heat conditions intensified once again in Kota.
Strong sunshine and rising temperatures affected normal movement during the day.
Alwar: Evening drizzle brings relief
After a hot and humid day, dense clouds gathered over Alwar in the evening. Light drizzle and strong winds were reported in several areas, bringing temporary relief from the heat.
Udaipur: Tourist spots see fewer visitors
Udaipur experienced intense heat on Sunday, with temperatures rising slightly from the previous day.
- Maximum temperature: 39.6°C
Tourist footfall remained low at popular attractions such as Fateh Sagar Lake, Saheliyon Ki Bari and Sajjangarh due to the afternoon heat.
Jodhpur: Temperature remains above 40°C
Jodhpur continued to experience hot weather.
The maximum temperature remained close to normal levels for this time of year.
Sikar: Temperature rises sharply
The weather remained clear in Sikar throughout Sunday, and temperatures increased by around 2.5°C compared to the previous day.
Maximum temperature: 38°C
Residents experienced a return of hot conditions despite rainfall on June 6.
